description:
Who are we?

The South Australian Tertiary Admissions Centre (SATAC) provides reliable, efficient, and innovative tertiary admissions services for institutions in SA and the NT. Each year, we manage thousands of applications, publish key information for students, and support pathways into higher education. We’re a not-for-profit organisation driven by strong values - Take the Lead, Do the Right Thing, Foster Community, Work as One, and Achieve Excellence.

Our people are central to what we do, and we pride ourselves on offering a supportive, collaborative, and flexible workplace. With a commitment to professional growth, wellbeing, and work-life balance - including flexible work options, the opportunity for a Workcation up to one month per year, and 10 days of Wellbeing Leave in addition to standard leave entitlements - you’ll be joining a team of great people who genuinely care about making a difference.

What is the role on offer?

With continued growth and exciting new projects underway, we’re excited to be growing our Software Engineering team.

Working under limited direction, the role of Software Engineer will design, develop, support, and implement IT solutions, with a particular focus on our Core System and SATAC’s Online Application System. In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with the Engineering Lead to deliver solutions that align with approved standards while maximising the value and performance of SATAC’s platforms.

What you’ll be working on:

• Designing, programming and implementing our IT systems using modern development frameworks.

• Developing application programming interfaces (APIs) and front-end applications based on architectural standards and patterns.

• Maintaining and enhancing existing full-stack systems to ensure alignment with evolving business needs.

• Ensuring software development is aligned with approved software development policies, roadmaps, architecture and occurs in a collaborative manner.

• Maintain and support the data integration platforms between SATAC, institutions and other stakeholders.

• Developing technical specifications and documentation, applying quality assurance practices to ensure the successful delivery of online applications.

• Contributing to a software development lifecycle that balances agility with a considered risk profile.

• Supporting Service Delivery in the investigation, assessment, implementation, and enhancement of service levels.

Enough about us, let’s talk about you!

You’re a collaborative problem-solver with a passion for building quality software that makes a real impact. Combining strong technical expertise with a willingness to learn, share knowledge, and adapt to evolving business needs, you bring a proactive, solutions-focused mindset and the ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.

• Demonstrated strong proficiency in Java and TypeScript, with hands-on experience in building scalable full-stack applications using modern frameworks, and a solid understanding of reactive programming principles.

• Demonstrated expertise in end-to-end test automation using Playwright, with a focus on reliability, maintainability.

• Demonstrated experience in CI/CD practices, including pipeline design, automated testing, and deployment using industry-standard tools.

• Familiarity with Microsoft Azure, particularly PaaS services, with the ability to design and deploy cloud-native applications.

• Familiarity working in a mono repo environment, supporting efficient collaboration, dependency management, and streamlined development workflows.

• Familiarity with additional programming languages is beneficial. Certifications or extensive hands-on experience in multiple languages are considered a strong asset.

• Teamwork and collaboration capabilities.

Formal qualifications or a combination of relevant experience and/or education and training relevant to the position will also be expected.

Enjoy an outstanding environment, including:

• A modern, well-equipped office in the CBD - easily accessible by public transport.

• Work from home flexibility to support balance and lifestyle.

• The opportunity to take a Workcation, where you can work from anywhere in the world for up to one month per year.

• 10 days of Wellbeing Leave per year and 17% super.

• A supportive, collaborative team culture where values are lived, not just spoken, and where you’ll work with great people who care about their colleagues and the community.

• The chance to contribute to a respected not-for-profit that plays a vital role in education across SA and NT.

If you want to change tomorrow, act today.

If this role sounds like the right fit, submit a cover letter telling us what makes you the right person for the job. Your current resume should also be attached to your application.

SATAC is proud to support the WE'RE EQUAL initiative and foster a safe, welcoming, and respectful

environment for everyone in our community – because when people feel seen, respected and included, everyone benefits. www.equalopportunity.sa.gov.au

For a confidential discussion regarding this position, contact Shaun Wallis via email shaun.wallis@satac.edu.au.

If you would like more information on the role and/or a copy of the position description, contact satacHR@satac.edu.au.
